title: Marina Wyss Complete Course Guide — 16 Chapters, Step-by-Step Reference
author: Kai

sections.topic: Marina Wyss AI Agents Complete Course — 16-chapter step-by-step reference guide. Pure course content: what Marina teaches, verbatim, chapter by chapter. Not an analysis of Hyo.
sections.finding: 16 chapters covering: (1) What Is an Agent — perceive/decide/act loop. (2) Autonomy Spectrum — 5 levels from manual to fully autonomous. (3) Context Engineering — role + task + memory + tools + knowledge injection. (4) Task Decomposition — independent observability, no compound steps. (5) Tool Use — typed interface registry separate from implementation. (6) Memory & Knowledge — dynamic memory vs static knowledge separation. (7) Reflection — structured self-assessment after every action. (8) Guardrails — input validation, output filtering, scope boundary enforcement. (9) Evaluation — GVU pattern (Generator-Verifier-Updater), one ground-truth metric per agent. (10) Planning — plan before acting, replan on unexpected results. (11) Multi-Agent Systems — orchestrator/worker/verifier topology. (12) Advanced Decomposition — temporal (research→implement→verify), spatial (parallel subtasks), conditional (branch-based). (13) Latency & Cost — model tiering, caching, async execution. (14) Observability — WHY logging at every decision branch. (15) Security — sandbox execution, input sanitization, scope enforcement. (16) Agent Economy — trust scoring, specialization, composability. Source: https://www.youtube.com/@MarinaWyss
sections.implications: Universal pre-build reference. Before designing any agent or capability, check the Quick Reference Card 10 questions. The holy bible (marina-wyss-ai-agents-guide) shows how these principles applied to Hyo specifically. This guide shows the principles themselves.
nextSteps: Wire Quick Reference Card into AGENT_CREATION_PROTOCOL.md as mandatory pre-build gate
Reference Chapter 3 (Context Engineering) when debugging agent output quality
Reference Chapter 9 (GVU Pattern) when designing any new agent evaluation metric
Reference Chapter 12 (Advanced Decomposition) when any pipeline task takes >5 minutes
sources: https://www.youtube.com/@MarinaWyss — Marina Wyss AI Agents Complete Course
